summary: Defence lawyers had asked for case to be thrown out, claiming Maduro's rights were violated following US abduction.

title: Deporting soldiers? Why immigrant veterans fear removal from the US
summary: Trump's push for mass deportation has heightened fears that immigrant veterans of the US military could be expelled.

title: Narayana Health plans ₹3,000 crore capex, eyes further hospital expansion in South, East: Group CFO Sandhya Sriram
summary: Narayana Health is set to expand its presence in South and East India. The hospital chain plans to add approximately 2,000 beds through new projects.  The company is investing Rs 3,000 crore in these developments. Growth strategies include improving patient throughput, adopting new technologies, and optimizing its payer mix.  Narayana Health is also exploring selective expansion opportunities in other regions.

title: Spotting the value of AI in Finance
summary: The Mythos and Agentic AI debates, are not just about one model or one company. They are about the next phase of financial AI, where systems evolve from assisting decisions to actively shaping them. This transition will inevitably require a rethink on governance frameworks, model accountability and auditability, particularly in high-stakes environments like banking and insurance.

title: Indian Institute of Accountancy? Early idea triggers debate on future of CA, CS, CMA education
summary: The idea, first floated by a parliamentary panel, has gained fresh traction following media reports suggesting that the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) is considering a new body, tentatively called the Indian Institute of Accounting, which could oversee examinations and curriculum design for Chartered Accountants (CA), Company Secretaries (CS) and Cost Accountants (CMA). However, the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) has clarified that such reports are not backed by any official confirmation from the Ministry, underlining the uncertainty around the proposal.

title: CFOs step up AI bets despite mixed early returns, focus shifts to speed and scale: Survey
summary: A majority of chief financial officers are increasing enterprise-wide AI spending, with 56% planning to raise investments by more than 15% this year, according to a Band & Co survey.

title: Exclusive: Govt mulls CAG-like body for listed firms, private banks, NBFCs
summary: While public sector undertakings already come under the CAG led framework, the proposal currently being examined by a high-level PMO panel seeks to extend a similar model first to publicly listed companies, private sector banks and NBFCs, where auditor appointments are currently driven by boards and shareholders.

title: Waaree Renewable eyes BESS, data centres; sees strong EPC pipeline for FY27: CFO Manmohan Sharma
summary: From the last five financial years, the company has more than doubled its revenue each year. Growing from Rs 162 crore in FY22 to Rs 351 crore in FY23, and to Rs 876 crore and Rs 1,598 crore in FY24 and FY25 respectively. Giving a peek into FY27, the CFO said that the company has an unexecuted order book of 2.8 GW, and it expects that the pace will continue.

title: Nagarro ropes in former HCL Tech CFO to lead finance function
summary: Nagarro SE has appointed Prateek Aggarwal as its new Chief Financial Officer (CFO) and Management Board member, effective May 1, 2026. With over 20 years of finance leadership experience in the technology sector, Aggarwal previously held CFO roles at HCLTech and Hexaware Technologies. His appointment is expected to support Nagarro's ambitious growth and AI transformation initiatives.

title: When AI runs the firm, who pays tax?
summary: The rise of artificial intelligence is reshaping our governance and taxation landscape. With AI systems becoming increasingly self-sufficient, there's a pressing need to reevaluate existing legal frameworks. While the current productivity spike from AI is advantageous for many workers, the next generation of intelligent AI might necessitate a complete overhaul of our tax policies.

title: US seizes Iranian cargo ship, Tehran vows to retaliate
summary: America has seized an Iranian cargo ship. Iran has vowed to retaliate. This action threatens the ongoing ceasefire. Peace talks are also in doubt. Iran will not participate in further negotiations. Global oil prices have reacted to the news. The war has severely impacted energy supplies. Tensions remain high in the region.

title: Iran conflict and global risks slow GCC expansion in India
summary: Global geopolitical tensions are impacting new technology center openings in India. While the number of new centers slowed, existing ones are expanding. Experts predict continued growth in greenfield centers if uncertainties ease. The GCC ecosystem is steadily rising, driven by new capabilities in AI and product engineering.

title: MPC minutes signal extended RBI pause as West Asia conflict clouds outlook
summary: The minutes of the April policy meeting, where the MPC unanimously held the repo rate at 5.25% and retained a neutral stance, indicate that policymakers are viewing the current inflationary pressures as supply-driven and are willing to look through first-round effects while preserving flexibility for future action

title: Nirmala Sitharaman, Piyush Goyal hold talks with Korean leaders on future bilateral cooperation
summary: India and South Korea are strengthening economic ties. Union ministers met with South Korean leaders and business executives. Discussions focused on financial sectors, startups, and trade expansion. Both nations aim to increase bilateral trade to 50 billion dollars by 2030. This collaboration will drive investment and innovation between the two countries.

title: Iran-US war: Strait of Hormuz disruptions have implications for India, says Rajnath Singh
summary: In Germany, he urged stronger India–Germany defence ties, expanded industrial cooperation, and coordinated responses to emerging security challenges including cyber, AI and drone warfare.

title: First tranche of India-US trade deal almost finalised: Piyush Goyal
summary: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said on Monday that the first tranche of the India-US bilateral trade agreement has almost been finalised, and a team of his ministry officials is in Washington for the discussions.

title: RBI plans to remain nimble to prevent persistent supply shock amid West Asia crisis
summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra addressed global challenges at Princeton University, stressing upon agile monetary policy to counter supply shocks from West Asia. The focus is on preventing inflation from becoming persistent. Prudent fiscal management and domestic production also play key roles, he said.

title: Gig workers social security scheme may have multiple fund managers
summary: One possibility under consideration is roping in the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) under the ministry to handle provident fund contribution and disbursement, while outsourcing pension to another fund manager.

title: Delhi to put its chips in semiconductor gambit with new policy push
summary: According to govt officials, the policy will be built around five key pillars: semiconductor design and intellectual property development, research and innovation, manufacturing-enabling segments such as assembly, testing, marking and packaging (ATMP) and OSAT, talent development, and a strengthened startup ecosystem.

title: Trump and a camel's dream: Where exactly are US-Iran talks headed?
summary: US President Trump claims a deal with Iran is imminent, with Tehran agreeing to surrender enriched uranium. However, Iran has sarcastically dismissed these assertions. Despite diverging narratives, Pakistan facilitates ongoing talks for a second negotiation round, aiming to de-escalate regional tensions and potentially secure an interim understanding.

title: Textiles Ministry plans duty cuts to shield industry amid West Asia crisis
summary: In a bid to navigate the turbulent waters of global shipping issues, India's textiles ministry is advocating for lower duties on rayon pulp and certain cotton varieties. Furthermore, there's potential for a postponement of anti-dumping duties on specific yarns, which would significantly aid supply chains.

title: India-UK free trade pact may come into force from second week of May: Official

title: West Asia War: Trump says 50% tariff threat on aiding Iranian military applies to China as well as peace talks stall

title: RBI's three major decisions to boost Ease of Doing Business: Explained
summary: The Reserve Bank of India on Wednesday introduces a set of measures aimed at enhancing Ease of Doing Business, including easing board level compliance requirements, consolidating supervisory instructions, and removing due diligence norms for MSMEs on trade platforms.

title: RBI MPC holds repo rate at 5.25%, maintains neutral stance
summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee has maintained the key policy repo rate at 5.25 percent, holding its neutral stance. This decision balances inflation concerns with domestic growth, supported by steady demand and fiscal measures. This marks the fourth consecutive pause in the current interest rate cycle.

title: RBI likely to maintain 'status quo' on repo rates, address key macro issues: Economists
summary: The RBI is set to announce the outcome of its first MPC meeting for FY 26-27 on Wednesday, April 8. The current repo rate stands at 5.25 per cent. It was last changed in December 2025, when the central bank had cut the repo rate by 25 basis points to 5.25 per cent.

title: Government issues draft norms to standardise state, district GDP estimates
summary: The key focus areas in SDP include identification and integration of new data sources such as administrative records, sector-specific databases and survey inputs to improve coverage and reliability, and refinement of estimation techniques to better capture emerging sectors, particularly services and the unincorporated segment.

title: RBI MPC Meeting: India seen holding rates as 'Goldilocks' phase gives way to stress
summary: Instead, the focus is likely to be on calming financial markets, with assurances of readiness to support the weakening rupee and inject liquidity to keep bond yields in check, economists and traders said.

title: RBI may keep rates unchanged, focus on rupee stability and bond yields
summary: The six-member monetary policy committee meets April 6-8 for the first time since the war broke out on February 28.

title: Import duty relief to bring down cost pressure, benefit downstream sectors: India Inc
summary: ​Industry chamber PHDCCI said the measure directly lowers input costs for downstream industries by removing customs duty, thereby reducing landed prices of imported feedstock and intermediates. This cost reduction is expected to lead to margin protection, particularly for sectors with high raw material intensity.

title: Jan Vishwas law will ease compliance burden on businesses: India Inc
summary: ​Parliament on Thursday passed the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026, to amend 784 provisions in 79 central laws for decriminalising and rationalising minor offences to further improve the business environment and check harassment of people.

title: RBI's net short position rises to $77 billion in Feb as it defends rupee
summary: The forward position for the month of March is likely to breach $100 billion, according to Bloomberg.
